title: Little known SC decisions
primary_topics: separation of powers as internal-process autonomy
sub_topics: Ko v. Republic (April 12, 2023) — legitimacy vs filiation
Republic v. Pryce (March 8, 2023) — senior 20% discount on interment
Ong v. Senate (March 28, 2023) — congressional inquiry limits and Pharmally
Soliman v. People (April 25, 2023) — alternative fine for online libel
4E Steel Builders v. Maybank (March 13, 2023) — foreign bank foreclosure
ACT Teachers v. Duterte (January 24, 2023) — Congressional Journal vs livestream
People v. Almayda (June 14, 2023) — warrantless drug-seizure inventory rules

stances.claim: Under the Family Code, the presumption of legitimacy of children born during marriage cannot be challenged by a biological father seeking to tag the child as his 'natural child' — the presumption is for the child's benefit, and even the mother is prohibited from declaring against the child's legitimacy.

Congressional inquiries are subject to four limitations: (1) 'in aid of legislation,' (2) per duly published rules, (3) respect for rights of persons appearing, (4) detention only until termination of inquiry — the Pharmally arrests violated the third when contempt was imposed 'without giving them the opportunity to be heard.'
stances.rhetorical_move: four-limitation doctrinal enumeration + application to specific case

stances.claim: The principle of separation of powers grants the three branches autonomy to determine their respective internal processes — the SC respects the Congressional Journal just as Congress respects the Court's internal deliberations and the Executive's internal Cabinet decisions.
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-symmetry framing of separation of powers + 'in my humble opinion' insertion

stances.claim: For online libel, courts may impose the alternative penalty of fine instead of imprisonment — Soliman v. People resolved this by noting the RPC penalty for traditional libel is 'prision correcional in its minimum and medium periods OR (repeat, or) a fine ranging from P40,000 to P1,200,000, or both.'

signature_phrases: If I may add my humble opinion
Even the mother is prohibited by law from declaring against the child's legitimacy
register_markers: THIRD, THE CONSTITUTIONAL POWER OF CONGRESS
ALL CAPS enumeration markers (First, Second, ... Seventh)
If I may add my humble opinion
decision_framework_signals: applies structural-symmetry of separation of powers (each branch respects others' internal processes)
uses enumerated-limitations framework (four limits on congressional inquiry) as doctrinal-test device
frames legal presumptions (legitimacy) as protective of vulnerable parties (children)
uses interpretive-emphasis device ('repeat, or') to surface alternative-penalty doctrine
